caption: A screen recording shows a web browser displaying a "List Randomizer" tool on the website RANDOM.ORG, which identifies itself as the "True Random Number Service." A yellow advisory banner warns, "We only operate services from the RANDOM.ORG domain. Other sites that claim to be operated by us are impostors." The page features a randomized list of numbers from 1 to 10. In the top right, a chat window displays messages from users "nogged" and "Nightbot," including a link to "the BREAK SCHEDULE, SHIPPING INFO and MORE >>> https://bit.ly/N2Sbe8Ky." At the bottom of the screen, a red and black banner for "CASEBREAKS.COM" is visible. As the video progresses, the page is refreshed, causing the randomized number sequence to change. The count of how many times the list has been randomized updates from "You have randomized this list 4 times" to "You have randomized this list 5 times."
